name: Vitamin C

tolerance.full: not applicable (essential nutrient, no receptor tachyphylaxis)

toxicities: Very low acute toxicity. Megadoses (>2000mg/day oral) may cause osmotic diarrhea, abdominal cramps, and increase the risk of calcium oxalate kidney stones in susceptible individuals.
Doses above 1000mg saturate the intestinal active transport system, leading to a sharp decline in bioavailability.
summary: Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) is an essential nutrient involved in the repair of tissue and the enzymatic production of certain neurotransmitters.
effectsSummary: Antioxidant effects, supports immune system function, assists in collagen synthesis, and improves iron absorption.
dosageRemark: The recommended dietary allowance (RDA) is around 75-90 mg per day for adults. Upper intake level is set at 2000 mg per day.
generalRisks: Gastrointestinal distress (diarrhea, nausea, cramps) when consumed in large megadoses.
longtermRisks: High intake may increase the risk of developing kidney stones in predisposed individuals.
saferUse: Do not exceed 2000 mg per day unless advised by a medical professional.
Ensure adequate fluid intake when taking high doses.
